title: Step 1: Extract TimeControls into standalone component

requirements.summary: Create a new standalone TimeControls component that encapsulates the time button and Real/Hourly/Yearly toggle controls from InteractiveGlobe.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: The component exports a default or named `TimeControls` React component
The component accepts props for all state values and handlers needed to control time mode (Real/Hourly/Yearly) and the time button
The component renders the time button and Real/Hourly/Yearly toggle controls visually identical to the existing bottom-right panel controls in InteractiveGlobe.tsx
`InteractiveGlobe.tsx` is NOT modified in this step
The file compiles without TypeScript errors

file_operations.description: Create a new standalone React component that encapsulates the time control UI (time button that toggles the time picker/mode and the Real/Hourly/Yearly toggle buttons). The component must accept props including: timeMode (string indicating 'real', 'hourly', or 'yearly'), onTimeModeChange (callback for changing time mode), isTimePickerOpen (boolean), onToggleTimePicker (callback to open/close time picker), currentTime (Date or equivalent), onTimeChange (callback for time changes), and any other state/handlers currently used for time controls in InteractiveGlobe.tsx. The component should render the same button layout and styling as the existing bottom-right time controls panel in InteractiveGlobe.tsx, using Tailwind classes and Lucide icons (Clock, Play, FastForward icons) to maintain visual parity. Include TypeScript prop types. Do not modify InteractiveGlobe.tsx in this file operation.
